content: I am a student and researcher, currently pursuing an MSc. at Utrecht University in the Netherlands. My research interests include real-time rendering in computer graphics, specifically ray tracing and path tracing. In 2023, I graduated from Purdue University with a BSc. in Computer Science. There, I was involved in performing research with the Computer Graphics and Visualization Laboratory. Contact me at e.a.h.kato@students.uu.nl.
summary: I am a student and researcher, currently pursuing an MSc.

content:
A real-time ray tracing engine in C++, implemented with a variety of acceleration structures, including bounding volume hierarchy (BVH), kd-tree, and grid structures.
Link to code.
summary: A real-time ray tracing engine in C++, implemented with a variety of acceleration structures, including bounding volume hierarchy (BVH), kd-tree, and grid structures.
title: Real-Time Ray Tracer

content:
A rasterization engine written with C++ and FLTK where I implemented a variety of rendering techniques, including pixel-accurate shadows with irregular z-buffering, and reflections in environment maps.
Link to code.
Link to writeup.
summary: A rasterization engine written with C++ and FLTK where I implemented a variety of rendering techniques, including pixel-accurate shadows with irregular z-buffering, and reflections in environment maps.
title: Custom 3D Rasterization Engine

summary: A project where I explore the Square-1, a Rubik’s cube variant puzzle. These experiments include implementing a graph representation of puzzle state subset known as OBL, developing an algorithm to generate transformations of a puzzle turn sequence, and more.
title: hashtagcuber.com

summary: I created a VR game for a research group at Purdue University’s School of Languages and Cultures, who are studying the role of VR in language learning. This game tasks the user with matching the target language sounds with its corresponding character, in this case Japanese language’s hiragana.
title: VR for Language Learning
